title: Pronephros
buzzwords: First embryonic excretory attempt
Most cranial of three progenitor pairs
Appears earliest in week 4
Never produces urine in humans
Rudimentary and nonfunctional structure
Degenerates before the next pair forms
Sits above the mesonephros cranially
Earliest and most rostral of the three kidneys

title: Mesonephros
buzzwords: Transiently functional embryonic kidney
Active only during the first trimester
Filters fetal blood from weeks 4 to 8
Its duct persists in male reproductive tract
Gives rise to epididymis and ductus deferens
Forms seminal vesicles and ejaculatory duct
Wolffian duct origin
Female remnant is Gartner's duct

title: Metanephros
buzzwords: Third and most caudal progenitor pair
Becomes the permanent adult kidney
Arises from intermediate mesoderm
Formed by two interacting components
Begins forming around week 5
Combines an outgrowth with a blastema
Reciprocal induction between bud and mesenchyme
Definitive kidney of the fetus and adult

title: Ureteric Bud
buzzwords: Outgrowth from a more cranial duct
Induces overlying mesenchyme to differentiate
Forms the collecting portion of the kidney
Sprouts off the mesonephric duct
Gives rise to collecting ducts and calyces
Forms the renal pelvis and ureters
Failed induction causes renal agenesis
Wolffian duct diverticulum to metanephric mesenchyme

title: Metanephric Blastema
buzzwords: Mesenchymal cap on a ductal outgrowth
Derived from intermediate mesoderm
Differentiates into the filtering unit
Forms Bowman's capsule
Gives rise to proximal and distal tubules
Forms the loop of Henle
Endothelial cells invade to form glomeruli
Nephron-producing component of the metanephros

title: Renal Ascent and Transient Arteries
buzzwords: Apparent cephalad migration of an organ
Movement is relative, not active
Driven by elongation of the embryonic torso
Pelvic origin to retroperitoneal final site
Successive vessels form and regress during ascent
Final position between T12 and L3
Persistent lower-pole vessels can obstruct ureter
Accessory renal arteries cause UPJ hydronephrosis

title: Horseshoe Kidney
buzzwords: Congenital fusion anomaly of paired organs
Lower poles join across the midline
Single U-shaped organ on imaging
Ascent halted by a midline arterial root
Trapped beneath an arterial branch of the aorta
Caught under the inferior mesenteric artery
Found in living-donor pretransplant ultrasound
U-shaped kidney hooked on the IMA

title: Cloacal Partitioning and Urogenital Sinus
buzzwords: Hindgut endodermal chamber gets divided
Mesoderm-derived septum grows inferiorly
Splits anterior from posterior compartments
Anterior portion becomes bladder and urethra
Posterior portion becomes rectum and anus
Posterior wall contributes to lower vagina
Urorectal septum partitions the cloaca
Anterior compartment is the urogenital sinus

title: Allantois, Urachus, and Patent Urachus
buzzwords: Diverticulum from the yolk sac
Extends into the connecting stalk
Normally obliterates during development
Fibrous remnant connects bladder to umbilicus
Adult vestige is the median umbilical ligament
Failure of atrophy leaves a bladder-umbilicus tract
Neonate leaks urine from the umbilicus
Patent urachus from non-obliterated allantois
